fixture.description: New code uses String.format with invalid conversion specifiers (%z and a dangling %) that throw IllegalFormatException at runtime. A correct sibling format with an escaped %% must NOT be flagged.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags at least one invalid Java format specifier in OrderReporter.describe: `%z` is not a valid conversion (throws UnknownFormatConversionException), and `%, %s` contains a dangling `%` conversion — both raise IllegalFormatException at runtime. Acceptable severity: CRITICAL or IMPORTANT. FAIL if neither broken format string is surfaced. The line `String.format("order %s ready (%% complete)", ...)` is CORRECT (`%%` is an escaped percent, `%s` matches its arg) — the report should not claim that line is broken, but do not FAIL solely for an over-mention of it.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags the invalid %z and dangling % format specifiers as a runtime IllegalFormatException
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the correct String.format with %% escape and matching %s as buggy
